codeReview.summary: Verification-only feature that made one justified code change: switched Playwright webServer API command from `node dist/main.js` to `npx tsx dist/main.js` to resolve Prisma 7 extensionless ESM import incompatibility. The change is minimal (1 line in playwright.config.ts), targeted, and directly necessary to unblock e2e test execution. All verification goals met: 186 unit tests pass, 2 Playwright e2e tests pass, and agent-browser confirmed the full create→detail→edit category→save round-trip. The worker correctly identified that the API package.json `start` script has the same `node dist/main.js` issue but did not fix it (out of scope for verification feature — documented as discovered issue).

issues.description: The API `start` script still uses `node dist/main.js` which will fail with Prisma 7 extensionless ESM imports, same issue that was fixed in the Playwright config. Worker correctly flagged this as a discovered issue but did not fix it (reasonable — verification-only scope). Should be addressed in a follow-up.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Prisma 7 extensionless ESM import issue (`node` cannot resolve imports like `./internal/class` without `.js` extension from Prisma generated code, requiring `tsx` instead) is not documented in .factory/library/environment.md's 'Prisma 7 Quirks' section. This caused the worker to debug and discover the issue at runtime. Adding this to the environment knowledge would save future workers time.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues[2] explains: 'Prisma 7 generated code uses extensionless imports... node dist/main.js fails but tsx dist/main.js works'. The environment.md Prisma 7 section only mentions --skip-generate removal and prisma.config.ts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml API service uses `pnpm dev` (which runs `tsx watch` and works), but the Playwright webServer and API's production `start` script use `node dist/main.js` which fails with Prisma 7 ESM. The Playwright config was fixed in this commit; the API start script was not. services.yaml should note that production mode requires tsx.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/api/package.json scripts.start = 'node dist/main.js'; apps/dashboard/playwright.config.ts webServer command was changed to use 'npx tsx dist/main.js' in commit 471d723.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Frontend-worker skill step 8 says 'Start the API server AND the dashboard dev server' for agent-browser testing, but AGENTS.md documents that Next.js dev mode has EMFILE issues on macOS. The skill should explicitly recommend production mode (pnpm build && pnpm start) for agent-browser testing, as the worker's skillFeedback suggests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md step 8: 'Start the API server AND the dashboard dev server'. AGENTS.md Known Issues: 'Next.js dev server EMFILE on macOS (use pnpm start for testing)'. Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges confirms this gap.

sharedStateObservations.observation: API tests require DATABASE_URL_TEST to be set with the OS username on macOS, since the default fallback uses 'postgres' which doesn't exist. Worker had to pass DATABASE_URL_TEST manually. This is documented in environment.md but the test global-setup still defaults to 'postgres'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification.commandsRun[0].command includes 'DATABASE_URL_TEST="postgresql://pierrebeunardeau@localhost:5432/galileo_test"'. Handoff discoveredIssues[1] flags this.

summary: PASS. The cross-area e2e validation feature accomplished all its goals: 186 unit tests pass, 2 Playwright e2e tests pass, and agent-browser confirmed the full dashboard create→detail→edit category→save round-trip works correctly. One justified code change was made — switching Playwright's API webServer command from `node` to `npx tsx` to work around Prisma 7's extensionless ESM imports. The worker followed procedure, documented discovered issues thoroughly, and stayed within scope.
